metadata.description: Scientifically-sourced modulator tables for synthetic athlete data generation and ML feature engineering.
metadata.usage: Consumed directly by the Python data generation script (data/generators/generate_fixtures.py). All numeric distributions assume Gaussian sampling clamped to [min, max].

Foster2001.authors: Foster C, Florhaug JA, Franklin J, Gottschall L, Hrovatin LA, Parker S, Doleshal P, Dodge C
Foster2001.title: A new approach to monitoring exercise training
Foster2001.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
Foster2001.key_finding: Session RPE (CR-10) × duration (min) = training load in Arbitrary Units (AU). Collected 30 min post-session.

Hooper1995.title: Monitoring overtraining in athletes: recommendations
Hooper1995.key_finding: Four-item questionnaire (sleep quality, fatigue, soreness, stress) each scored 1-7. Hooper Index = sum (4-28). >20 signals overreaching.

Gabbett2016.title: The training-injury prevention paradox: should athletes be training smarter and harder?
Gabbett2016.key_finding: ACWR > 1.5 associated with 2-4× increased injury risk. Sweet spot: 0.8-1.3.

Buchheit2014.title: Monitoring training status with HR measures: do all roads lead to Rome?
Buchheit2014.key_finding: HRV (RMSSD) is sensitive to training-induced fatigue. Individual-referenced daily monitoring outperforms population norms.

Dellal2010.authors: Dellal A, Chamari K, Wong DP, Ahmaidi S, Keller D, Barbe R, Carling C
Dellal2010.title: Comparison of physical and technical performance in European professional soccer match-play: FA Premier League and La Liga
Dellal2010.key_finding: GPS metrics by position in La Liga and EPL. Midfielders cover most total distance; wingers most high-intensity distance.

Bradley2009.authors: Bradley PS, Sheldon W, Wooster B, Olsen P, Boanas P, Krustrup P
Bradley2009.title: High-intensity running in English FA Premier League soccer matches
Bradley2009.key_finding: Position-specific GPS benchmarks in the EPL. Sprint threshold: 25.2 km/h. High-intensity threshold: 19.8 km/h.

Armstrong1994.authors: Armstrong LE, Maresh CM, Castellani JW, et al.
Armstrong1994.title: Urinary indices of hydration status
Armstrong1994.key_finding: Urine specific gravity (USG) benchmarks: <1.010 well-hydrated; 1.010-1.020 minimal dehydration; >1.030 serious dehydration.

session_types._note: RPE distributions collected 30 min post-session on modified Borg CR-10 scale (0-10). sRPE = RPE × duration_min in Arbitrary Units (AU). Use Gaussian sampling clamped to [min, max].

recovery.description: Active recovery, pool hydrotherapy, light mobility and stretching

technical.description: Ball mastery, passing combinations, set piece rehearsal

tactical.description: Positional shape work, pressing triggers, defensive/offensive transitions

physical.description: High-intensity running, intermittent sprint intervals, aerobic-anaerobic conditioning

match_prep.description: Day-before activation session, final set piece walk-through

wellness_modulators._note: Additive integer adjustments to Hooper scale values (1-7, 1=best, 7=worst). Applied based on calendar context. Values are clamped to [1, 7] after addition. Multiple contexts stack additively.

normal_training_day.note: Baseline reference. All other contexts are relative to this.

match_day.note: Pre-match anxiety elevates stress and disrupts sleep quality. Effect observed from morning of match.

day_1_post_match.note: Acute neuromuscular fatigue and muscle damage peaks at 24h post-match. Buchheit 2014: HRV suppressed ~16% at this timepoint.

day_2_post_match.note: Delayed-onset muscle soreness (DOMS) continues into day 2.

day_3_post_match.note: Partial recovery. Residual soreness for higher-minute players.

travel_day.note: Long-haul travel disrupts circadian rhythm (sleep) and elevates psychological stress. Applicable to intercontinental fixtures.

rest_day.note: Reduced training load promotes recovery. Clamped to minimum value of 1 on each dimension.

consecutive_high_load_streak.note: Cumulative fatigue accumulation. Applied in addition to day-specific modifiers.

recovery_modulators._note: Multipliers applied to individual hrv_baseline_ms from player_baseline_profiles. Additive adjustments to resting_hr_bpm_baseline. Applied post-match or post-high-load session.

day_1.note: Acute sympathovagal imbalance. Buchheit 2014 meta-analysis: mean RMSSD suppression of ~16% at 24h post-match.

day_2.note: Partial parasympathetic reactivation. ~10% HRV suppression persists at 48h.

day_3.note: Near-baseline restoration for most players at 72h. Residual ~5% suppression.

day_4_plus.note: Full recovery assumed by day 4 in absence of additional high-load stressors.

high_load_training_day.note: Non-match high-load session causes acute HRV suppression similar in magnitude to match but shorter duration.

dehydration_penalty.note: Dehydration independently elevates resting HR via plasma volume reduction and suppresses HRV. Armstrong 1994; indirect cardiac autonomic effect.

gps_load_by_position._note: Match-day GPS reference values per playing position. Training session values derived by applying training_load_multipliers below. Gaussian sampling recommended: mean ± std, clamped to [mean-2*std, mean+2*std].

goalkeeper.source_note: GK-specific estimates; Dellal2010 and Bradley2009 excluded GKs from main analysis.

center_back.source_note: Dellal2010 La Liga/Serie A data; lowest HID proportion of outfield positions.

fullback.source_note: Bradley2009 EPL: fullbacks show highest sprint counts among defenders due to wide overlap runs.

wingback.source_note: Estimated from fullback + winger averages in 3-5-2/3-4-3 systems; higher HID than fullback.

defensive_midfielder.source_note: Dellal2010: DMs cover highest total distance of all positions, but lower sprint intensity than wider roles.

central_midfielder.source_note: Bradley2009: CMs cover the most total distance per match of all positions in EPL study.

attacking_midfielder.source_note: Dellal2010: higher HID proportion vs box-to-box due to penetrating runs behind defensive line.

winger.source_note: Bradley2009: highest sprint counts of any position in EPL study. Wide attacking positions drive most high-velocity actions.

second_striker.source_note: Interpolated from Dellal2010 attacking positions. Similar to winger but more central movement.

striker.source_note: Dellal2010: lower total distance than midfielders due to positional holding play; high-intensity movement concentrated in sprint bursts.

training_load_multipliers._note: Applied to match-day values to estimate GPS output per session type. Based on typical training-to-match intensity ratios in elite football literature.

acwr_thresholds._calculation_formula: ACWR = (7-day sRPE sum) / (28-day sRPE mean × 4). Denominator ×4 normalises the 28-day mean to a 4-week cycle for direct comparison with the 7-day acute window.
acwr_thresholds._note: Risk multipliers are relative to the optimal zone (1.0 = reference injury rate). Values from Gabbett 2016 Figure 4.

undertraining.interpretation: Monotony zone. Insufficient training stimulus may reduce fitness and paradoxically increase injury risk from deconditioning. Recommend progressive load increase.

optimal.interpretation: Sweet spot (Gabbett 2016). Progressive overload within adaptive capacity. Target for weekly planning.

caution.interpretation: Elevated risk. Reduce next session's planned RPE. Monitor wellness scores closely.

danger.interpretation: Danger zone. Gabbett 2016: 2-4× injury risk increase vs optimal zone. Immediate load reduction recommended.

critical.interpretation: Critical spike. Strongly recommend modified training or rest. High probability of non-contact soft tissue injury within 7 days.

xgboost_risk_thresholds._note: Probability thresholds for risk_category assignment from XGBoost output. Calibrated on historical injury data.

physiological_ranges._note: Valid bounds for CHECK constraints (schema.sql) and synthetic data generation clamping. All values derived from cited literature and clinical standards.

sleep_duration_h.source: Mah et al. 2011 (Sleep journal); general sports science consensus for elite athletes

sleep_quality.unit: Hooper scale (1=very very good, 7=very very bad)

fatigue.unit: Hooper scale (1=very very good, 7=very very bad)

soreness.unit: Hooper scale (1=very very good, 7=very very bad)

stress.unit: Hooper scale (1=very very good, 7=very very bad)

sprints_count.unit: count of sprint efforts above 25.2 km/h

accel_decel_count.unit: count of acceleration/deceleration events above ±3 m/s²
